message:
Fix terminal escape sequence leaks with Ghostty and Starship

- Disable OSC color query responses in Ghostty to prevent background
  color detection responses from leaking as visible garbage
- Disable Starship's transient prompt feature which causes cursor
  position query responses to leak due to async timing races

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -93,7 +93,10 @@ if status is-interactive
   # Setup a fancier prompt.
   if command -sq starship
     starship init fish | source
-    enable_transience
+    # Note: enable_transience disabled due to terminal query race conditions.
+    # Starship's transient prompt uses cursor position queries (DSR/CPR) that
+    # can leak as visible garbage (e.g., ^[[3;1R) when responses arrive async.
+    # enable_transience
   end
 
   # Launch gpg-agent for use by SSH.

@@ -1,6 +1,11 @@
 # Shell
 shell-integration = fish
 
+# Disable OSC 10/11/12 color query responses. When enabled (default), programs
+# like Starship query terminal colors for theme detection, but the responses can
+# leak as visible garbage (e.g., ^[]11;rgb:ffff/ffff/ffff^\) due to timing races.
+osc-color-report-format = none
+
 # Colors.
 theme = dark:GitHub Dark Default,light:GitHub Light Default
 #background = #111111
